chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1227 of 2018 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE (S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 07.04.2021 Mr. Nasrullah Unar, Advocate for the respondents No.6 and 7. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. == None present on behalf of petitioner. Learned counsel for the respondents No.6 and 7 draws attention of this Court’s order dated 30.05.2019 as reproduced hereunder:- “Mr. Unar Submits that after filing of petition, the petitioner has disappeared and consequently mutation with regard to transaction of revenue of rights has been disturbed and thus respondents are facing hardships. He next submits that neither the petitioner nor his counsel are pursuing the case vigilantly as lastly the petition was fixed before the Court on 08.05.2018 when it was noticed for 09.08.2018; however, on 09.08.2018 due to rush of work it could not get turn and consequently went discharged. He insists that right from 09.08.2018 neither the petitioner nor petitioner’s counsel have took pain to get it fixed before the Court by filing any application. Such their conduct shows that they have no concern but to linger on the proceedings, so that respondents may be put under boiled water. Hence, pleads urgency. In view of above, urgency is granted. To come up on 03.07.2019 with notice to respondents and direct notice to the petitioner as well intimation notice to his counsel.” As there have been serious challenges with regard to maintainability of the instant petition, coupled by the fact that the counsel has chosen to stay away from the proceedings; accordingly, the petition is dismissed.

In view of above, urgency is granted. To come up on 03.07.2019 with notice to respondents and direct notice to the petitioner as well intimation notice to his counsel.” As there have been serious challenges with regard to maintainability of the instant petition, coupled by the fact that the counsel has chosen to stay away from the proceedings; accordingly, the petition is dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish Steno*
